attacking_style: Ultra-long range attacks from breakaways, typically 50-35km out on climbs. Exceptional diesel engine for sustained solo efforts when in form, but descent positioning and power reserves often fail to sustain gaps on technical terrain.
team_tactics: EF Education-EasyPost provides early positioning for breakaway infiltration but limited high-mountain support. Healy operates as protected option in Ardennes/stage hunting roles, exposed when crashes eliminate him as team leader.
terrain_preference: Medium-length climbs (Salita Capuccini-style) where he can collect KOM points and launch 50km attacks. Strong in hilly Ardennes classics (2nd Amstel, podium at Brabantse). Struggles on flat roads and technical descents where he cannot sustain gaps or loses positioning.
vulnerability: Poor energy management on flat roads before climbs (lost KOM jersey twice after premature attacks). Insufficient power reserves to close gaps on descents. Sprint weakness forces tactical gambling from chase groups. Crash risk in technical finales.
key_patterns: Attacks 50km out from breakaways on climbs, repeated across multiple Giro stages (at least 4 instances)
Targets KOM jersey aggressively, collects points from early breaks but exhausts reserves defending (3+ occasions)
Loses sustained gaps on descents despite strong climbing attacks, lacking diesel on technical downhills (3 observations)
Full-gas efforts on flat/early terrain backfire, causing energy depletion and jersey losses (2 documented instances)
Exceptional form when diesel engine firing: won by 1:49 margin, biggest in 5 years
Late-race positioning weakness in sprints forces solo attacks from chase groups despite power deficit
form_trajectory: positive: Demonstrated exceptional progression during 2023 Giro with historic 1:49 margin victory and KOM jersey capture, though energy management errors and crash in Ardennes week show tactical refinement still needed
